key_themes: Resilient growth in deposits (+11% to £4.57bn) and FUMA (+21%) with strengthened capital, liquidity, and higher dividend despite 31% PBT decline to £24.2m
Significant Chairman & CEO insider buying (£2.6m / 300k shares at 860p) and stock trading at ~50-52% of NAV with attractive ~6-7% yield
Analyst views (Hardman & Co and consensus) point to 2026 profit stability, higher EPS (~169p), higher-for-longer rates tailwind, and price targets of 1,200p (material upside from ~850p)
sources_checked: X posts (Hardman & Co, investors, market updates)
Price action and analyst target data (Yahoo, MarketBeat)
summary: Arbuthnot Banking Group delivered resilient 2025 results with strong deposit and wealth management growth, robust balance sheet metrics, and an increased dividend, even as pre-tax profit fell 31% to £24.2m from lower rates, high surplus liquidity, and some one-off items. The Chairman & CEO’s large open-market share purchase at 860p, combined with the stock trading at roughly half of NAV and analyst forecasts for 2026 stability plus a 1,200p target, reflect clear value and confidence. The biggest risk is sustained net interest margin pressure if rates remain lower for longer or liquidity stays elevated.[[1]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/arbuthnot-banking-group-lonarbb-hits-new-1-year-low-should-you-sell-2026-04-16/)[[2]](https://www.tipranks.com/news/company-announcements/arbuthnot-banking-group-delivers-resilient-2025-as-deposits-and-assets-grow-despite-profit-dip)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/chairman-ceo-arbuthnot-banking-group-074727256.html)[[4]](https://hardmanandco.com/research/corporate-research/arbb-2025-lower-margins-2026-profit-stability/)

recent_posts.highlight: 2025 results show strong deposit/wealth growth and specialist lending; margins squeezed by rates but 2026 profit stability expected
CEO bought ~£2.8m at 860p post-results; PER ~8.9, ~6.6% yield, trading at 52% of net assets
Undervalued 190-year merchant bank at 52% of tangible book; sticky deposits, overcapitalized, heavy insider buying
Stock reached new 52-week low (~775p) with recent price weakness below moving averages
